data.title: Yinhawangka Aboriginal Corporation and Rio Tinto sign Interim Modernised Agreement in Perth, Australia.
data.description: In Perth, Australia, the Yinhawangka Aboriginal Corporation and Rio Tinto have recently signed an Interim Modernised Agreement, signaling a significant development in their enduring partnership. This interim agreement expands upon the 2013 Participation Agreement between the Yinhawangka People and Rio Tinto. It outlines the collaborative efforts and lays the groundwork for a comprehensive modernised agreement that will dictate Rio Tinto's activities on Yinhawangka Country in the future. This agreement represents an important step forward in the relationship between the parties.
Venezuelan Leader Maduro Says Tanker Held by US Had 1.9 Million Barrels of Oil

data.title: RealD & CJ 4DPLEX collaborate to enhance cinematic experience. Regal Cinemas to upgrade 52 sites for Avatar release.
data.description: Regal Cinemas has announced its plan to enhance 52 locations ahead of the release of Disney/Lightstorm's film Avatar: Fire and Ash, set to hit theaters on December 19th. The upgrades will occur in various locations including Louisville, Colo., and Burbank, Calif. RealD, a top 3D cinema technology company, is collaborating with CJ 4DPLEX, known for their innovative work, to carry out the enhancements.

data.title: Travel industry urged to double down on digital transformation as cyber breaches escalate. Cybersecurity a top focus, with 66% of airlines and 73% of airports prioritizing it. Average cost of security breach estimated at $4.44 million for large global companies. Sector urged to adopt cloud-native infrastructure for operational resilience.
data.description: The aviation and travel industry faces challenges with legacy IT systems amid increasing passenger volumes and cyber threats. Senior VP at SITA, Martin Smillie, states urgent adoption of secure, scalable, cloud-native infrastructure is necessary for operational resilience. SITA's White Paper on SITA Connect Go emphasizes these benefits. A SITA report shows 66% of airlines and 73% of airports prioritize cybersecurity, with an average cost of security breaches estimated at $4.44 million. The industry must modernize IT infrastructure and migrate to cloud-native platforms to address these threats. The full article can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: SEC approves DTCC pilot for recording US securities on select blockchains using 'registered' wallets. Three-year program allows central clearinghouse to tokenize entitlements under modified regulatory oversight.
data.description: A program spanning three years will empower the market's central clearinghouse to develop tokenized entitlements under revised regulatory supervision.

data.title: HCIactive in Columbia announces AI-driven security modernization for 2025-2026, focusing on privacy & compliance.
data.description: In Columbia, Maryland, a recent development has emerged where security measures are being revolutionized through the use of artificial intelligence. This advancement aims to enhance privacy, compliance, and system integrity in an innovative manner. The incorporation of AI technologies is set to significantly improve security protocols.

data.title: HCIactive launches VIRA Audit Bot to advance TPA and carrier audits, improves accuracy and eliminates manual processes.
data.description: In Columbia, Maryland, HCIactive has introduced the VIRA Audit Bot, an innovative AI-powered audit engine tailored for enhancing vendor and broker remittance audits in the insurance industry. Operating within HCIactive’s AI First and AI Everywhere framework, the VIRA Audit Bot streamlines processes, enhances audit precision, and provides immediate validation across TPA, carrier, payroll, and remittance channels. Through cutting-edge technology, this solution marks a significant advancement in transforming audit practices within the insurance sector.

data.title: SEMIFIVE expands with new subsidiary in Japan for AI chip design projects, enhancing global presence.
data.description: Expanding its proficiency in high-performance custom chip design for AI and HPC, SEMIFIVE has recently launched a subsidiary in Japan to fortify its worldwide presence. The company, headquartered in Seoul, South Korea, made this strategic move on December 11, 2025. SEMIFIVE is known for its expertise in creating custom AI semiconductors, specifically ASICs, and aims to enhance its global reach through this expansion.

data.title: BMTC GROUP INC. reports $24,405,000 revenue increase to $474,724,000 for Oct 31, 2025 period. Net earnings drop to $14,313,000 from $29,419,000 in 2024.
data.description: The company's revenues for the nine-month period ending October 31, 2025, increased by $24,405,000 to $474,724,000 compared to $450,319,000 for the same period in 2024, reflecting a 5.4% growth. The growth was mainly driven by a 6.2% revenue increase in the commercial sector from the Tanguay division, while the real estate division saw a 95.9% decrease in investment property revenue. Net earnings for the period were $14,313,000, down from $29,419,000 in 2024. The company completed the disposal of an asset generating a gain of $2,765,000. The Tanguay division contributed a net profit of $24,399,000. Operational improvements and restructuring initiatives helped mitigate the impact of severance costs and outsourcing decisions. Adjusted net earnings for the period amounted to $14,313,000, showing a decrease when compared to the same period in 2024. Additionally, the company made significant changes to its distribution and warehousing operations in the Greater Montréal area during the period.
